pi_items.source: Task description specifies all 8 columns and their calculations. Each column is required for quota attainment calculation: temporal join (QuotaStartDate/QuotaEndDate), sum aggregation (ActualSales), and percentage calculation (AttainmentPct). All are standard SQL patterns.
Confirmed via Microsoft Learn official documentation and GitHub releases page. This is the official Microsoft distribution of AdventureWorks2022.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic sample data - fictional company 'Adventure Works Cycles'
pi_items.source: GitHub microsoft/sql-server-samples repository documentation confirms AdventureWorks is synthetic sample data for teaching database design, not real-world data
Web search results confirm BusinessEntityID 275 is Michael Blythe, Sales Representative, Territory Northeast

privileged_info_summary: The task uses AdventureWorks2022, an official Microsoft synthetic sample database representing a fictional bicycle manufacturing company. The test case uses person ID 275 (Michael Blythe, Sales Representative), a salesperson with a quota of $300,000 in territory Northeast. The specific test date 2011-05-31 exists in the SalesPersonQuotaHistory table. The view must calculate quota attainment by performing temporal range joins between SalesPersonQuotaHistory and SalesOrderHeader tables, using LEAD() to derive quota period end dates. The required 8-column view schema (BusinessEntityID, FirstName, LastName, QuotaStartDate, QuotaEndDate, SalesQuota, ActualSales, AttainmentPct) directly implements the task requirements. CSV export must filter for year 2012 quota periods only.
